title: Forgotten Crops Could Feed the Future
subtitle: 被遗忘的作物或成未来粮仓

paragraphs.en: On a small fruit farm near the Straits of Malacca, Lim Kok Ann is down to just one tree growing kedondong, a crunchy, sour berry that Malaysians mostly use in pickles and salads. 'It's not very well-known,' says the 45-year-old, who is instead focusing on longan berries and pineapples, which have bigger markets. 'We have to grow what is profitable.' But less than an hour away in the Malaysian countryside, scientists inside three giant silver domes are trying to change the future of food by growing and processing so-called alternative crops — such as kedondong.
paragraphs.zh: 在马六甲海峡附近的一个小果园里，林国安只剩下一棵加东酸果——一种马来西亚人主要用来做泡菜和沙拉的脆酸浆果。这位45岁的果农说：“它不怎么出名。”他转而专注于市场更大的龙眼和菠萝。“我们必须种能赚钱的东西。”但在不到一小时车程的马来西亚乡村，科学家们在三个巨大的银色圆顶内正试图通过种植和加工所谓的替代作物——比如加东酸果——来改变食物的未来。

paragraphs.en: At the headquarters of global research centre Crops For the Future, this under-used fruit has been turned into a sugar-free juice, high in vitamin C and getting top marks in sensory evaluations. 'Anything you see here is a forgotten crop,' says Sayed Azam-Ali of the abundant plants weaving through the gardens. Prof Azam-Ali explains that just four crops — wheat, maize, rice and soybean — provide two-thirds of the world's food supply. 'We're dependent on these four,' he says. 'But actually there're 7,000 crops we've been farming for thousands of years. We ignore all of those.'
paragraphs.zh: 在全球研究中心“未来作物”的总部，这种未被充分利用的水果已被制成无糖高维C果汁，并在感官评估中获得高分。“你在这里看到的一切都是被遗忘的作物，”赛义德·阿扎姆-阿里指着花园中繁茂的植物说。阿扎姆-阿里教授解释说，仅四种作物——小麦、玉米、水稻和大豆——就提供了世界粮食供应的三分之二。“我们依赖这四种，”他说，“但实际上有七千种作物我们已经种植了数千年。我们忽略了它们全部。”

paragraphs.en: Researchers are trying to unlock the potential of these ignored crops — plants they describe as forgotten or under-used as they are displaced by increasingly uniform diets fuelled by processed ingredients from the major crops. It's a timely quest. The food sector is already responsible for nearly a third of global greenhouse gas emissions. By 2050 the world must produce 50 percent more food to feed the projected global population of 10 billion. Meeting this demand without contributing to climate change calls for urgent solutions.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员正试图释放这些被忽视作物的潜力——它们因主要作物加工成分驱动的日益同质化饮食而被取代，被称为被遗忘或未充分利用的作物。这是一场及时的探索。食品行业已占全球温室气体排放的近三分之一。到2050年，世界必须增产50%的粮食才能养活预计的100亿全球人口。在不加剧气候变化的情况下满足这一需求，需要紧急解决方案。

paragraphs.en: Forgotten crops hold key answers. By investing in neglected local plants, countries can reduce their reliance on imported crops and their carbon-heavy supply chains. Bringing back the variety of crops humans once ate also boosts food security at a time warming climates threaten existing crops. On top of that, forgotten crops are among the most climate-resilient and nutritious, argues Azam-Ali. His summary is plain: 'Dietary diversification is critical to the future of humanity.' Food security experts agree.
paragraphs.zh: 被遗忘的作物蕴藏着关键答案。通过投资被忽视的本土植物，各国可以减少对进口作物及其高碳供应链的依赖。在气候变暖威胁现有作物之际，恢复人类曾食用作物的多样性也能增强粮食安全。此外，阿扎姆-阿里认为被遗忘的作物是最能适应气候变化且营养最丰富的作物之一。他的总结很简单：“饮食多样化对人类的未来至关重要。”粮食安全专家表示赞同。

words.meaning: adj. 有弹性的，能复原的

paragraphs.en: Azam-Ali knows that scepticism firsthand. He came across alternative crops in the 1980s through the work of women farmers he met in Niger. The then-PhD student remembers marvelling at the crops they grew in their backyards, without the benefits of technology, to feed their families when the big crops failed. He saw a tremendous opportunity to build alternative food systems. But 'the resistance was enormous,' he recalls. Undeterred, he ploughed on. Project after project helped prove these crops were viable as alternatives to the staple ones.
paragraphs.zh: 阿扎姆-阿里深知这种怀疑。20世纪80年代，他通过在尼日尔遇到的女农民的工作接触到替代作物。当时还是博士生的他记得，自己惊叹于她们在后院种植的作物——无需技术帮助，就能在主要作物歉收时养家糊口。他看到了建立替代食品系统的巨大机遇。但“阻力巨大，”他回忆道。他没有被吓倒，继续耕耘。一个接一个的项目帮助证明这些作物作为主要作物替代品是可行的。

words.meaning: adj. 未被吓倒的，不屈不挠的

paragraphs.en: But the question of whether these crops would be marketable remained. 'That's the critical thing,' he says. In one of the centre's domes, food technologist Tan Xinlin uses powdered moringa leaves in place of some wheat flour to bake a cake lower in gluten and higher in nutrients. Tan's job is to create recipes with these unfamiliar ingredients that will appeal to both local and international tastes. In recent years she has used forgotten crops such as moringa and bambara groundnut to make everything from instant soup to Indian snacks.
paragraphs.zh: 但这些作物是否有市场销路的问题依然存在。他说：“这才是关键。”在该中心的一个圆顶内，食品技术员谭新林用辣木叶粉代替部分小麦粉，烘焙出低筋高营养的蛋糕。谭的工作是用这些尚不熟悉的原料创造出吸引本地和国际口味的食谱。近年来，她使用辣木和班巴拉花生等被遗忘的作物制作从速食汤到印度小吃的各种食品。

paragraphs.en: It's a strategy to appeal to the world's growing middle classes who are increasingly turning to the fast and processed food industries. It's also a way to help counter perceptions of local crops as 'old or poor people's food' or as inferior 'women's crops', adds Tan. The roots of these connotations can run deep. The bambara groundnut, a protein-rich native crop of sub-Saharan Africa, can trace its marginalisation to colonial rule. 'African women who grew bambara groundnut were actually punished for growing it,' says Azam-Ali.
paragraphs.zh: 这一策略旨在吸引世界上不断壮大的中产阶级，他们正越来越多地转向快餐和加工食品行业。谭补充道，这也是帮助扭转人们对本土作物“老人或穷人的食物”或劣等“女性作物”看法的一种方式。这些内涵的根源可能很深。富含蛋白质的撒哈拉以南非洲本土作物班巴拉花生，其边缘化可追溯到殖民统治。阿扎姆-阿里说：“种植班巴拉花生的非洲妇女实际上会因此受到惩罚。殖民列强说你们不能种那个，因为没有油，没有市场。”

paragraphs.en: But today the bambara murukku is one of CFF's best reviewed foods and they are aiming to get it into grocery stores, pointing to the success of crops like quinoa to potential investors. Some 30 years ago, quinoa was virtually unheard of outside its native mountains in Bolivia and Peru. Today the nutritious grain is found on the menus of lavish restaurants across the world. Measuring crops by nutrition instead of yield is at the heart of the forgotten foods enterprise.
paragraphs.zh: 但如今班巴拉花生脆条已成为CFF评价最高的食品之一，他们正致力于将其引入杂货店，并向潜在投资者展示藜麦等作物的成功。大约30年前，藜麦在玻利维亚和秘鲁的原生山区以外几乎闻所未闻。如今这种营养谷物出现在世界各地豪华餐厅的菜单上。以营养而非产量来衡量作物，是被遗忘食品事业的核心。

paragraphs.en: Ever since the 'green revolution' of the 1960s, high-yielding crops have dominated modern agriculture. Today 'nutrition is becoming a time bomb', says Azam-Ali, as growing carbon dioxide levels strip crops of their minerals. Instead of bio-fortifying major crops we should be investing in those forgotten crops that are already more nutritious, he asserts. In the bowels of CFF's third dome, lab manager Gomathy Sethuraman reveals vines of winged beans growing under bright yellow light, where scientists study the impact of higher temperatures and carbon dioxide on the nutritional make-up of alternative crops.
paragraphs.zh: 自20世纪60年代“绿色革命”以来，高产作物一直在现代农业中占据主导地位。如今阿扎姆-阿里说，“营养正在成为一颗定时炸弹”，因为不断上升的二氧化碳水平剥夺了作物的矿物质。他断言，我们不应只对主要作物进行生物强化，而应投资于那些本就更有营养的被遗忘作物。在CFF第三个圆顶的深处，实验室经理戈马西·塞图拉曼展示在明亮黄光下生长的四棱豆藤蔓，科学家正在那里研究更高温度和二氧化碳水平对替代作物营养成分的影响。

paragraphs.en: This research is 'the game changer', says Azam-Ali, ensuring that 'future crops' are also the healthiest ones in warmer climates. There is a growing global momentum around forgotten foods, says Danielle Nierenberg of Food Tank. Besides CFF, key groups championing agricultural diversity include Crop Trust, Slow Food, Icrisat and Bioversity International. Add to that more middle-income consumers searching for nutritious foods and others eager to try the unprocessed foods their grandparents once ate.
paragraphs.zh: 阿扎姆-阿里说，这项研究“改变了游戏规则”，确保“未来作物”在更温暖气候下也是最健康的。Food Tank的丹妮尔·尼伦伯格表示，全球范围内围绕被遗忘食品的势头正在增强。除CFF外，倡导农业多样性的重要团体还包括作物信托、慢食、国际半干旱热带作物研究所和国际生物多样性组织。此外，更多中等收入消费者正在寻找营养食品，另一些人则渴望尝试祖辈曾食用的未加工食品。

paragraphs.en: But the rising interest in forgotten foods in some quarters is overtaken by the global spread of Western-style diets heavy in sugar, fat and processed foods in others. A key obstacle to promoting fading local crops in Malaysia, for example, is 'the obsession with imported products', says Jenifer Kuah, co-founder of a restaurant that champions locally-sourced food. Some analysts say the shift to forgotten foods is inevitable. 'Climate change is going to mean tastes are forced to change,' says Tim Lang. We 'have to get used to eating other crops' as yields of staple crops fall.
paragraphs.zh: 但在某些地区，对西式高糖高脂加工饮食的全球性追捧，盖过了部分人对被遗忘食品日益增长的兴趣。例如在马来西亚推广衰落本土作物的一个主要障碍是“对进口产品的痴迷”，吉隆坡一家倡导本地食材餐厅的联合创始人珍妮弗·夸说。一些分析人士认为向被遗忘食品的转变是不可避免的。蒂姆·朗说：“气候变化将意味着口味被迫改变。”随着主要作物产量下降，我们“必须习惯吃其他作物”。
